mnemonics: CRUD Buddy: Create, Read, Update, Delete. These are the four core operations you'll perform on your list buddy.
Traverse the Train: Think of a linked list like a train. To find a car (node), you must start at the engine (head) and walk through each car, you can't jump straight to the caboose (tail).
keyPoints: Insertion and Deletion are efficient at the head (O(1)) but inefficient at the tail without a pointer (O(n)).
Traversal is sequential; you cannot access a node by its index directly as you can with an array.
The 'next' pointer of the last node must always be set to NULL to mark the end of the list.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to update the 'next' pointers correctly during insertion or deletion, which can break the list or cause memory leaks.
Attempting to access data from a node that has already been freed (using a dangling pointer after deletion).
Not handling the special case of an empty list (where head == NULL) when writing operations like insertion at the head.
